description: Curated sources that feed GYRD living updates. Each source maps to rules/agents it affects.

sources.name: Claude Model Releases
sources.description: New Claude models change instruction following, tool use, and reasoning. Rules that compensate for model behavior may become redundant or need strengthening.

sources.name: Claude Code CLI Updates
sources.description: Claude Code adds hooks, agent features, and changes file format. Rules may need to reference new capabilities.

sources.name: Cursor IDE Updates
sources.description: Cursor changes MDC rule format, adds agent features. Format adapters may need updating.

sources.name: OpenAI Codex CLI
sources.description: Codex CLI reads AGENTS.md. Format changes affect generated output.

sources.name: OWASP Security Updates
sources.description: Security landscape changes. Review rules should reflect current threat models.

sources.name: Anthropic Engineering Blog
sources.description: Best practices for building with Claude. Harness design, context engineering, evaluation patterns.

assumptions: Sequential pipeline is optimal for current model capabilities
Fresh code-reviewer prevents self-praise loops
Max 3 iterations is the right cap before escalation
Model-per-tier mapping (opus=oversight, sonnet=workers) is cost-effective
Context window degradation starts around 32K tokens
Dedicated tools (Read/Grep/Glob) are better than Bash equivalents
One task per conversation produces better output
OWASP Top 10 is the right security checklist for code review
npm audit + signatures catches supply chain attacks
Lockfile discipline prevents dependency hijacking
Three-persona review (Saboteur, New Hire, Security) covers all angles
80% confidence threshold prevents false positives
Plain language output serves both dev and non-dev users
Running tests > reading code for verification
7-step mechanical checklist catches most issues
Named failure patterns (6) cover common bugs
Rules decay when model/tool behavior changes
Testable assumptions are better than vague guidelines
Source → rule mapping enables automated update detection
Skills and agents exist as first-class abstractions in the tool
Checking available agents before manual work improves quality
Decision tree (agent > skill > direct) is the right priority
MDC format (.mdc files) is Cursor's rule format
Cursor reads .cursor/rules/ directory on session start
Format is stable enough to generate reliably
AGENTS.md is the cross-agent standard format
Codex CLI and other tools read this file
Format spec is stable (Linux Foundation)
